Junior: What is the difference between a bug and a defect?
How do you write a basic test case? Walk me through an example.
What types of testing do you know? Give a brief description of each.
How do you communicate a bug you found to a developer?
Tell me about a time you worked with a teammate to solve a problem.
How do you prioritize which tests to run when time is limited?
What tools have you used for bug tracking or test management?
Mid: What is the difference between verification and validation?
How do you decide what to automate vs. test manually?
Describe your approach to writing a test plan for a new feature.
What is regression testing and when would you run it?
How do you handle a situation where a developer disagrees with a bug you reported?
Explain your experience with API testing.
How do you measure test coverage and what metrics do you track?
Senior: How do you define a QA strategy for a new project from scratch?
Describe how you would build and mentor a QA team.
How do you balance quality gates with delivery speed in an agile environment?
What is your approach to risk-based testing?
How do you drive quality culture across engineering teams?
Describe a time you identified a systemic quality issue and how you addressed it.
How do you evaluate and introduce new testing tools or frameworks?

Junior: What is the difference between frontend and backend development?
Explain what an API is and how you've used one.
How do you approach debugging when something isn't working?
Describe a project you built or contributed to. What was your role?
How do you handle feedback on your code during a review?
What version control tools have you used and how?
How do you stay organized when working on multiple tasks?
Mid: Explain the difference between REST and GraphQL.
How do you handle database migrations in a production environment?
What strategies do you use for API rate limiting?
Describe how you would debug a performance bottleneck in an API endpoint.
What is the difference between horizontal and vertical scaling?
How do you handle state management in a large application?
Explain the difference between controlled and uncontrolled components in React.
Senior: How do you design a system architecture for a new product?
Describe your approach to technical debt — how do you identify and prioritize it?
How do you make build vs. buy decisions for infrastructure components?
What is your strategy for ensuring reliability and observability in production?
How do you mentor junior developers while maintaining your own delivery commitments?
Describe a time you led a major refactoring effort. How did you manage risk?
How do you approach cross-team technical alignment and standards?

Junior: What is test automation and why is it important?
What programming languages have you used for writing tests?
Describe the difference between unit tests and integration tests.
How do you approach learning a new testing tool or framework?
Tell me about a time you collaborated with a developer to fix a bug.
What is a test framework? Name one you've worked with.
How do you handle a test that sometimes passes and sometimes fails?
Mid: What is your approach to building a test automation framework from scratch?
How do you integrate automated tests into a CI/CD pipeline?
Explain the testing pyramid and how it influences your strategy.
How do you handle flaky tests?
Describe how you would test a microservices architecture.
What is contract testing and when would you use it?
How do you decide the right level of test coverage for a feature?
Senior: How do you design a test infrastructure that scales across multiple teams?
Describe your approach to shift-left testing in a large organization.
How do you measure and communicate the ROI of test automation?
What is your strategy for testing in production safely?
How do you drive adoption of testing best practices across engineering teams?
Describe a time you had to overhaul an existing test suite. What was your approach?
How do you balance test maintenance costs with coverage goals?

Junior: Tell me about yourself through the projects, experiences, or challenges that shaped you the most as an engineer.
Tell me about your most recent project, the problem you were solving, your specific role, and what you personally contributed.
Tell me about a difficult technical or project challenge you faced, how you approached it, and what the outcome was.
Describe the best engineering team you've worked with, what made it successful and what was your role in that success?
Tell me about a time something failed, a bug, incident, or missed expectation. What happened, what was your role, and what changed afterward?
Tell me about a time you had unclear requirements or incomplete information, how did you move forward and make decisions?
Tell me about a skill, habit, or area where you struggled before but intentionally improved.
What motivates you as an engineer, and what type of work or environment brings out your best performance?
Tell me about a project or problem where you felt truly engaged, what made it meaningful for you?
Imagine I call your current teammates tomorrow. What would they say is the best part of working with you, and what might they find challenging?
Mid: Walk me through your engineering journey, what decisions, projects, or moments have really influenced where you are today?
Imagine I'm joining your team tomorrow. Walk me through your latest project, how the team worked and what parts you influenced or owned.
Describe a situation where things did not go as planned, how did you identify the problem, contribute to the solution, and what did you learn?
Tell me about how you collaborate with teammates when solving complex problems or making technical decisions.
How do you keep your team and stakeholders informed when work is moving fast or when things are unclear?
Describe a situation where you took ownership of a problem that wasn't fully your responsibility.
Describe a situation where you had to solve a problem without having all the answers upfront,m how did you approach it?
Describe a time when feedback changed the way you work, what did you learn, and what actions did you take?
Tell me about a time your impact went beyond your own tasks and helped other engineers or the team improve.
What are you looking for in your next role, and what kind of environment helps you grow and do your best work?
What is something about your working style that genuinely helps your team, and something you're still working to improve?
Senior: What types of problems or projects have defined your career so far, and what are you looking for in your next challenge?
Tell me about a project you're proud of. What made it successful, and what exists today because of your contribution?
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a technical decision. How did you communicate your perspective and handle the outcome?
Tell me about a time you helped improve teamwork, communication, or collaboration within your team.
Tell me about a mistake you made in your work, how did you handle it, communicate it, and prevent it from happening again?
Tell me about a time expectations changed during a project, how did you adapt and keep the work moving?
Tell me about something you recently learned on your own, and how you applied it.
Describe a situation where you influenced a technical decision, process improvement, or standard without being directly responsible for it.
If you joined a team with low ownership, quality issues, and unclear processes, how would you approach improving it?
Looking back at your recent experience, what are you most proud of, and what would you like to do better going forward?
